text: Which version of Java introduced records?
What keyword is used to declare a sealed class?
Which of these is a valid pattern matching expression introduced in Java 16?
What does a record automatically generate?
Which interface does a sealed class use to list permitted subtypes?
What Java feature allows exhaustive matching over sealed types in a switch?
Which of the following is NOT a permitted subtype keyword for sealed types?
What is the primary purpose of virtual threads (Project Loom, Java 21)?
options: Enable high-throughput concurrency without blocking OS threads

text: Which Java 21 feature allows deconstruction of a record inside a switch case?
What constraint does a compact record constructor enforce that a normal constructor does not?
options: It cannot assign fields directly — components are assigned implicitly after the body

text: Which Java version finalized text blocks (triple-quoted strings)?
Which API creates a new virtual thread for each submitted task in Java 21?
What does the yield keyword do inside a switch expression block?
options: Returns a value from the switch expression
Breaks out of the switch without a value
Passes control to the next case

text: Which interface introduced in Java 21 adds getFirst(), getLast(), and reversed() to the collections hierarchy?
What keyword was finalized in Java 22 (JEP 456) to explicitly declare a variable whose value is intentionally unused?
What is the most common cause of virtual thread pinning to its carrier thread?
options: Entering a synchronized block or method

text: What is the primary purpose of StructuredTaskScope in Java's structured concurrency API?
options: To replace CompletableFuture with a simpler chaining API
To enforce that all forked subtasks complete before the scope's owner exits it
To provide automatic retry logic for failed tasks
To cap the number of virtual threads in flight

text: When pattern matching for switch covers all permitted subtypes of a sealed interface, what can the compiler guarantee?
options: Runtime type checks are skipped for better performance
The switch is exhaustive and no default case is required
Each case must match exactly one concrete subtype
Guarded patterns are evaluated before unguarded ones

text: Which statement about Java records and inheritance is correct?
options: Records can extend other records but not regular classes
Records are implicitly final and implicitly extend java.lang.Record, so they cannot extend any other class
Records can extend abstract classes as long as those classes have no fields
Records support single inheritance from classes and multiple inheritance from interfaces

text: In pattern matching for switch (Java 21+), what keyword introduces a guard condition on a pattern?
What does StructuredTaskScope.ShutdownOnFailure do when any forked subtask fails?
options: Retries the failed subtask up to three times before propagating
Ignores the failure and waits for all remaining tasks to complete
Shuts down the scope, cancels remaining subtasks, and rethrows the first failure on join()
Logs the failure and returns a partial result from successful subtasks

text: Which API finalized in Java 22 (JEP 454) provides a safe alternative to JNI for calling native code and accessing off-heap memory?
Why are virtual threads unmounted from their carrier thread during blocking I/O?
options: To allow the JIT compiler to optimize the blocking call
So the carrier thread can run other virtual threads while the I/O completes, maximizing throughput
To avoid sharing heap memory between the virtual thread and the OS
Because blocking I/O requires a dedicated OS thread that bypasses the carrier

text: In the Java virtual thread model, what is a carrier thread?
options: A virtual thread that spawns and coordinates other virtual threads
A platform (OS) thread from the ForkJoinPool that mounts and executes virtual threads
A thread that propagates context variables to child threads via InheritableThreadLocal
A lightweight wrapper thread dedicated to I/O operations

text: Why should ReentrantLock be preferred over synchronized blocks when writing virtual-thread-friendly code?
options: ReentrantLock has lower overhead than synchronized in all cases
synchronized is deprecated as of Java 21
synchronized pins the virtual thread to its carrier thread for the duration of the lock, blocking the carrier from running other virtual threads
ReentrantLock integrates with the virtual thread scheduler to avoid blocking

text: How does ScopedValue differ from ThreadLocal in the context of structured concurrency?
options: ScopedValue values are mutable and shared between threads; ThreadLocal values are not
ScopedValue is immutable within a scope and efficiently inherited by child threads without per-thread copying
ScopedValue persists beyond its binding scope whereas ThreadLocal requires manual removal
ScopedValue requires explicit removal to prevent memory leaks; ThreadLocal does not

text: What does the Gatherers API (finalized in Java 24, JEP 485) enable that Collectors cannot?
options: Parallel stream collection with custom merge functions
Custom stateful intermediate stream operations, not just terminal collection
Lazy evaluation of entire stream pipelines
Collecting into concurrent data structures with lock-free semantics

text: What does record pattern deconstruction in a switch (Java 21+) allow that a simple instanceof pattern does not?
options: Matching record types without requiring an explicit cast afterwards
Binding multiple record components directly in the pattern, including nested record deconstruction, in a single expression
Checking whether a record reference is null before matching
Matching records based on their declaration order without naming components

text: Which scenario is a poor fit for virtual threads?
options: Handling tens of thousands of concurrent HTTP requests
Executing blocking JDBC queries against a relational database
Running CPU-intensive tasks such as image processing or cryptographic computation
Reading large files from a slow network file system

text: What happens when null is passed as the selector expression in a pattern matching switch (Java 21+)?
options: It always falls through to the default case
It matches a case null label if present; otherwise NullPointerException is thrown
It is automatically treated as matching the first case in the switch
It causes a compile-time error unless an explicit null check precedes the switch
